subject: Cloud Computing
title: Vendor Lock-In Tactics
description: A quick win: understand Vendor Lock-In Tactics.

hook.text: Cloud providers want you to stay forever. Every proprietary service is a thread that ties you tighter.

buildup.text: AWS Lambda, Azure Active Directory, and Google BigQuery all have no direct equivalent on other clouds.

discovery.text: Data egress charges punish leaving. Moving a petabyte out of AWS costs roughly $90,000.

twist.text: Some lock-in is worth it. Proprietary services are often better than generic alternatives.

climax.text: The key is choosing lock-in deliberately — knowing the cost of leaving before you commit.

punchline.text: Lock-in isn't always bad. Accidental lock-in always is.

quiz.question: How do data egress charges contribute to cloud lock-in?
options: They make moving data out of a provider extremely expensive
They charge extra for uploading data to the cloud
They limit the amount of data you can store
